title: Anthropic identifies industrial-scale distillation attacks by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax
why_it_matters: Anthropic caught three major labs creating 24,000+ fraudulent accounts and 16M+ exchanges to distill Claude's capabilities. This is the first public disclosure of industrial-scale model theft and has huge implications for API providers and model security.

title: GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro are rolling out now in ChatGPT
why_it_matters: OpenAI's latest model drop unifies reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows into a single model. Available in both ChatGPT and the API/Codex, this is a major competitive move against Claude and Gemini.

title: Introducing Claude Code Security — scans codebases for vulnerabilities and suggests patches
why_it_matters: Claude Code now includes a security scanner that finds vulnerabilities and suggests targeted patches for human review — going beyond traditional SAST tools by understanding code context. Limited research preview available now.

title: Detecting and preventing distillation attacks
why_it_matters: The technical companion to Anthropic's distillation disclosure — details how they detected the attacks and what countermeasures they're deploying. Essential reading for anyone running model APIs.

title: New in Claude Code: Remote Control — kick off tasks in terminal, control from your phone
why_it_matters: Claude Code sessions can now be monitored and controlled from the Claude mobile app or web while running on your machine. This decouples agent supervision from being at your desk.

title: Quantifying infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals
why_it_matters: Anthropic shows that infrastructure configuration alone can swing agentic coding benchmarks by several percentage points — sometimes more than the gap between top models. This calls into question tight leaderboard rankings.

title: Nano Banana 2: Pro-level image generation at Flash speed
why_it_matters: Google DeepMind's latest image generation model delivers Pro-quality output (world knowledge, subject consistency) at Flash-tier speed and cost. A significant price/performance shift for production image generation.

title: Karpathy: Programming fundamentally changed in the last 2 months — coding agents basically work now
why_it_matters: Karpathy argues there was a step-change in December — coding agents went from not working to working, driven by model quality improvements in coherence and tenacity. An important signal from one of the most credible voices in AI.

title: Voice mode rolling out in Claude Code — /voice to try it
why_it_matters: Claude Code gets voice interaction, rolling out to ~5% of users now. You can talk to your coding agent instead of typing, which changes the workflow for debugging and brainstorming sessions.

title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite — most cost-efficient Gemini 3 model yet
why_it_matters: Google drops its cheapest Gemini 3-series model, targeting high-volume production workloads. This intensifies the race to the bottom on inference costs across all major providers.

title: GLM-5 by Zhipu AI (zai-org) — text-generation model trending on HuggingFace
why_it_matters: Zhipu AI's GLM-5 is generating significant community interest with 1,713 likes and 210K downloads. A new open-weight contender in the text generation space from a major Chinese AI lab.

title: Statement from Dario Amodei on Anthropic's discussions with Department of War
why_it_matters: Anthropic publicly addresses its engagement with the Department of War. A significant policy statement from a leading safety-focused lab that sets precedent for how AI companies navigate government partnerships.

title: Qwen3-Coder-Next by Qwen — coding-focused text-generation model
why_it_matters: Qwen drops a coding-specialized model with over 1M downloads already, signaling aggressive competition in the open-weight coding model space. Pairs well with the Qwen3.5 fine-tuning guide also trending today.

title: Agentic engineering anti-patterns: things to avoid
why_it_matters: Simon Willison documents common mistakes in agentic AI engineering — the kind of practical guide that saves teams weeks of debugging. Essential reference as more teams adopt agent architectures.
action: Audit your agent code against these

title: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy: Version 3.0
why_it_matters: Version 3.0 of Anthropic's RSP updates the framework governing how they scale increasingly capable models. Sets the bar for industry self-regulation and signals what safety commitments Anthropic is making as models get stronger.

title: MiniMax-M2.5 — text-generation model by MiniMaxAI
why_it_matters: MiniMax releases M2.5 with 354K downloads and strong community reception — notable given MiniMax was just named by Anthropic as one of the labs conducting distillation attacks. The model stands on its own merits regardless.

title: Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide by Unsloth
why_it_matters: Practical step-by-step guide for fine-tuning the latest Qwen3.5 models using Unsloth. High HN engagement suggests strong developer demand for hands-on Qwen fine-tuning resources.
action: Follow the guide to fine-tune Qwen3.5

title: Something is afoot in the land of Qwen
why_it_matters: Simon Willison analyzes the shakeup at Qwen — key team members leaving amid rapid model releases. Important context for anyone building on Qwen's open models about the team's stability and direction.

title: PageAgent — a GUI agent that lives inside your web app (by Alibaba)
why_it_matters: Alibaba open-sources an in-browser AI agent framework that can interact with web app UIs directly. Unlike external browser automation, this embeds the agent within the app itself — a different paradigm for web automation.
action: Try embedding in your web app

title: Relicensing with AI-assisted rewrite
why_it_matters: A developer used AI to rewrite an entire codebase to change its license — raising novel questions about copyright, derivative works, and whether AI-rewritten code constitutes a new work. High HN engagement signals this touches a nerve.
